response: Alex has embraced a new reading habit, exploring different perspectives on love and relationships. This shift in reading has opened up a world of new ideas and experiences for Alex. Each novel that Alex has read has challenged their previous beliefs in a unique way. The first novel Alex read was a significant step in this journey. It contained a total of 120 pages, which Alex completed in just 4 days. The time it took to finish this book was not long, but it was meaningful. The second novel Alex read was longer in both length and complexity. It had 180 pages, which Alex took 6 days to read. This novel required more time and deeper reflection. The third and final novel Alex read was the longest of the three, with 240 pages. It took Alex 8 days to finish this book. Each novel had its own unique story and message. Alex read the same number of pages every day for each book. This consistency helped Alex maintain a steady reading pace. The daily reading routine was a key factor in completing each novel. The first novel had 120 pages divided over 4 days. That means Alex read 30 pages each day. The second novel had 180 pages spread over 6 days. This resulted in 30 pages being read daily. The third novel had 240 pages over 8 days, which also meant 30 pages per day. This pattern shows that Alex read the same number of pages each day for all three novels. The consistency in reading speed was impressive. The total number of pages read across all three novels is 120 plus 180 plus 240. That adds up to 540 pages in total. The total number of days spent reading all three novels is 4 plus 6 plus 8. That equals 18 days in total. To find the average number of pages read per day, divide the total pages by the total days. 540 divided by 18 equals 30. Therefore, Alex read 30 pages per day on average. This average is consistent across all three novels. The same daily reading rate was maintained for each book. This shows that Alex's reading pace was steady and reliable. The daily reading routine helped Alex complete each novel in a timely manner. The first novel was completed quickly, but it was still a meaningful read.
